name: Comedonal acne
dek: Open and closed comedones, primarily on the T-zone and chin. Not inflammatory, not painful, but the most common reason adults reach for a salicylic toner — usually the wrong one. Built around retinoid discipline, not exfoliation theatre.
bottom: A boring, retinoid-led protocol with a 12-week ramp will out-perform any toner-rich, scrubbing-heavy routine you can build. The only thing the trend is doing is selling you bottles.

triggers.n: The brown / black colour is oxidised sebum, not dirt. Scrubbing makes the inflammation worse.
Small, flesh-coloured, slightly raised. Often felt before they are seen — the gritty texture under fingertip.
If lesions are red or sore, you have moved into the inflammatory acne category — different protocol.
Particularly heavy oils, isopropyl myristate, and certain silicones. Non-comedogenic labelling is imperfect but useful.

ingredients.name: Adapalene 0.1%
ingredients.evidence: The OTC retinoid of choice for comedonal acne. 12-week ramp; pea-sized.

ingredients.name: Tretinoin 0.025%
ingredients.evidence: Faster comedolytic action than adapalene; less tolerable in the first 6 weeks.

ingredients.name: Salicylic acid 2%
ingredients.evidence: Best as a leave-on or 60-second cleanser; not as a daily toner.

ingredients.name: Azelaic 10%
ingredients.evidence: Pregnancy-safe; gentler ramp than retinoids; slower endpoints.

ingredients.name: Niacinamide 4–10%
ingredients.evidence: The supporting active. Useful daily; not a primary treatment.

phases.b: Cleanser, lightweight gel moisturiser, fluid SPF. Stop scrubs, stop physical exfoliators, stop pore strips.
Adapalene 0.1% pea-sized, 3 nights a week, on dry skin. Buffer with moisturiser.
Alternate nights for 4 weeks, then nightly. Add salicylic 2% as a 60-second AM cleanser if needed.
Most comedonal cases will plateau by month 4 on adapalene. Continue indefinitely; reduce to 4 nights / week if needed.
